what_it_does: An acceptance test that exercises the end-to-end space-keyed embedding path: it seeds vectors through a stub embedder, writes them into projection spaces via embed_corpus_to_spaces, reads them back through find_relations to verify near-but-not-far inversions, checks validation/fail-loud behavior, tests embedder-down degradation, and verifies that run_role surfaces finish_reason through an optional meta out-param without changing the returned shape.
observations: Uses deterministic stub embeddings instead of a live model because :8001 is unavailable.
Constructs two orthogonal per-space geometries (principles vs topics) so that find_relations(near=principles, far=topics) should return only A, excluding B and C.
Asserts vectors land at store.space_address(source, space), the same key find_relations reads.
Inspects the source of embed_corpus_to_spaces to prove it delegates to build_index(space=) and does not call .put_vector( directly.
Re-embedding unchanged records reports skipped rather than embedded, relying on build_index's content-hash diff.
Missing source_address, empty text, missing projection, or non-embeddable projection raise ValueError before any write.
Embedder failure sets degraded=True, writes no vectors, emits a durable warning event, and does not crash.
Patches fabric.transport.openai_transport to test the finish_reason out-param without a live LLM.
Asserts embed_corpus_to_spaces is absent from RHM_VERBS and emits no op.run / dispatch / resolve events.
standouts: Geometry-driven acceptance of cross-space inversion (near∩¬far) with explicit teeth cases B and C.
Source-code inspection assertion that embed_corpus_to_spaces reuses build_index(space=) rather than inventing a second vector path.
Transport patching to validate the O3 finish_reason out-param contract on the default run_role path.
conventions: Creates isolated FsStore instances under tempfile directories and cleans them up in finally blocks.
Uses a global PASS counter plus helper functions check() and raises() for assertion bookkeeping.
Restores patched module attributes in finally blocks.
Imports some dependencies inside test blocks to keep the patch seam local.
concerns: Source-code inspection of embed_corpus_to_spaces is brittle to formatting or internal refactoring.
Direct monkey-patching of fabric.transport.openai_transport is fragile if the module structure changes.
The check `store.index_corpus(...) and [...]` relies on the first list being truthy; if it were empty the expression would short-circuit to the empty list.
The test assumes the projection registry already declares principles and topics as embeddable.
notes: A wiring acceptance test that proves the corpus embed path populates queryable spaces and feeds find_relations, while guarding fail-loud and degrade semantics.
questions: What is the exact implementation and location of embed_corpus_to_spaces?
How is RHM_VERBS populated and what does membership signify?
What is the precise contract between build_index and FsStore.put_vector?
How does the real OpenAI transport populate meta finish_reason and usage?
What production trigger is intended to run this embed pass over live corpus records?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to close the end-to-end gap where embedded records were never put into a queryable space, and the file does exactly that by testing the embed path, find_relations consumption, validation, degradation, and the finish_reason out-param.
apparent_themes: Reuse existing persistence paths instead of parallel writes
Keep store writes off the MCP verb / engine run-record floor
Cross-space relational inversion via vector geometry
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish and protect a single, reusable, space-keyed corpus embedding path that correctly feeds the relation-finder, degrades safely when the embedder is unavailable, and stays outside the engine's verb/run-record layer.
novelty: It acceptance-tests a cross-space semantic inversion using deliberately orthogonal stub vectors across two spaces, and uses source inspection to enforce that persistence is delegated to an existing build_index path rather than a new direct put_vector call.
contribution: A runnable acceptance specification that documents the expected behavior of the space-keyed embed path, its integration with find_relations, its validation and degradation contracts, and the finish_reason out-param convention.
summary_for_embedding: An end-to-end acceptance test for embedding corpus records into projection spaces and verifying that find_relations can read them back to produce near-but-not-far inversions, with fail-loud validation, embedder-down degradation, and finish_reason out-param coverage.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to close and guard the surface-flagged gap where run_role embed output was not persisted into queryable spaces by proving the embed_corpus_to_spaces path writes, validates, degrades, and feeds relation search correctly.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that increments a global pass counter on success.
symbols.description: Takes a label and a condition, asserts the condition with a FAIL label on failure, and increments the module-level PASS counter on success.

symbols.does: Helper that checks a callable raises an expected exception.
symbols.description: Invokes the provided function and passes if it raises the specified exception; otherwise reports failure through check.

symbols.does: Builds a fresh Suite with an FsStore and discovered node registry.
symbols.description: Creates a temporary FsStore under the given root, discovers nodes from the project nodes directory, and returns a Suite instance.

symbols.does: Factory that returns a stub embed_fn keyed by input text.
symbols.description: Builds an embed function matching the build_index seam, returning a vector from the supplied lookup table for each input string.

symbols.does: Closure that performs the table lookup for stub embeddings.
symbols.description: The inner embed function produced by _stub; it receives transport, inputs, model, and dim, and returns one vector per input by looking up the text in the provided table.

symbols.does: Stub embed_fn that always raises to simulate a down embedder.
symbols.description: Used in the embedder-down degradation test; it raises RuntimeError to trigger the warning-and-degrade path.

symbols.does: Minimal Pydantic output model for the run_role probe.
symbols.description: A tiny Pydantic BaseModel with a single answer field, used as the output schema for the O3 finish_reason probe role.

symbols.does: Minimal role object for testing run_role finish_reason surfacing.
symbols.description: A simple stand-in role with id, op, prompt_template, output_schema, and input_addresses, used to invoke run_role without a real registry entry.

symbols.does: Factory that patches openai_transport to populate meta without a network call.
symbols.description: Returns a fake transport function that, when called, writes finish_reason and usage into the meta out-param exactly as the real transport would.

symbols.does: Closure that simulates a completion and fills the caller's meta dict.
symbols.description: The inner fake completion function; it returns a JSON response and populates the meta dict with finish_reason and token usage if meta is provided.
